notes: Blundell reports ~33% acute compensation. Value 0.50 is a conservative upward estimate for chronic settings — not directly extracted from data. NEAT/AT caps non-binding so share does not differentiate delta_fm predictions.

primary_source: Melanson et al. 2013 [10]; Flanagan et al. 2024 [13]
notes: Proportion 0.30 is author modelling assumption based on qualitative reading. No single study provides this exact fraction. Cap never binding so value does not affect delta_fm.
Pontzer & Trexler 2026 not confirmed as existing. If unavailable use Rosenbaum & Leibel 2010 [18] to justify AT contribution with at_share=0.15.
